channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 3 big predictions for AI in financial services in 2025
item.description: As we look ahead to 2025, the financial services landscape will be shaped not by AI as a standalone innovation, but by how it enables smarter, faster, and more personalized experiences. Here are three key predictions for how AI will transform the industry: 1.
item.dc:creator: Ben Borodach, Fast Company

item.title: One reason the U.S. government is so spooked by TikTok: AI
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here. The real reason the U.S. government wants to ban a ByteDance-owned TikTok TikTok could shut down in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Biden’s clean AI infrastructure plan could be hanging by a thread
item.description: An executive order from outgoing US president Joe Biden aimed at advancing the nation’s leadership in AI infrastructure is “welcome and needed in theory, but this one seems rushed and somewhat at odds with the order just passed on AI chip export restrictions,” an analyst said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: President Biden’s new executive order aims to bolster national security
item.description: President Joe Biden issued an executive order on Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation’s cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: It's a real shame but the world's first eGPU with Thunderbolt 5 interface won't run on Apple Silicon
item.description: Sparkle eGPU Studio-G 850 boosts data speeds to 120 Gbps with Thunderbolt 5, delivering faster performance and compatibility for demanding discrete desktop graphics cards.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: SASE 2025: Impact grows despite adoption hurdles
item.description: Secure access service edge technologies will garner a lot of attention in 2025, according to a recent survey, but despite the many benefits SASE promises, network and security leaders face an equal number of challenges in successfully implementing the technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Surprise finding sheds light on what causes Huntington's disease, a devastating fatal brain disorder
item.description: Scientists are unraveling the mystery of what triggers Huntington’s disease, a devastating hereditary disorder that strikes in the prime of life.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ungar, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nvidia intros new guardrail microservices for agentic AI
item.description: Nvidia today added new Nvidia inference microservices (NIMs) for AI guardrails to its Nvidia NeMo Guardrails software tools.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden cyber EO seeks proof of security from software vendors
item.description: The new cybersecurity executive order seeks to put teeth behind the secure software development standards that federal vendors are supposed to be following. The post Biden cyber EO seeks proof of security from software vendors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: From Safety to Accountability: The Game-Changing Impact of Kazakhstan’s Surveillance System
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump will 'find a way' to preserve TikTok, Waltz says
item.description: President-elect Trump will take action to preserve TikTok upon taking office, his incoming national security adviser said Wednesday, with a deadline looming for the app’s owner to sell or face a ban in the United States. Rep. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta to lay off more than 3,600 workers in latest shakeup
item.description: Meta is planning to lay off 5% of its workers, a total of more than 3,600 employees, as the company continues its focus on artificial intelligence and smart glasses.
item.dc:creator: David Matthews, The Mercury News

item.title: Mac malware mayhem as 100 million Apple users at risk of having personal data stolen
item.description: New Mac malware variant targets browser credentials and personal data, researchers find. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives tips to safeguard your Mac from the latest malware threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SEC sues Elon Musk, saying he didn't disclose Twitter ownership on time before buying it
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has sued billionaire Elon Musk, saying he failed to disclose his ownership of Twitter stock in a timely manner in early 2022, before buying the social media site.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At CES, PC makers aim for business, highlight AI-ready hardware
item.description: Enterprise computers are often the ugly ducklings of the PC world, viewed as dull, slow and less feature-rich than their consumer counterparts.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: HPE Aruba targets retailers with IoT, edge computing launches
item.description: HPE Aruba Networking has rolled out a variety of new products for retailers that are aimed at boosting connectivity for in-store and IoT networks, improving analytics, and streamlining management.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The devops certifications tech companies want
item.description: Devops continues to expand in development environments everywhere from small startups to the largest global enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path
item.description: CISA’s National Initiative for Cybersecurity Careers and Studies helps cyber pros keep up with technology changes ranging from AI to zero trust. The post CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5
